zh.Bingham, Nottinghamshire, England: 英國諾廷加姆爾賓厄姆三世
zh.Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, England: 英國多瑟特梅爾可姆 霍爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Bingham於大約1265年出生在英國多瑟特梅爾可姆 霍爾時，他的父親Robert Bingham35歲，母親Emma Or Eleanor Wake22歲。他於1290年與Parnelle Warmwell在英國英格蘭多瑟特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1317年在英國英格蘭享年54歲。

zh-hans.Bingham, Nottinghamshire, England: 英国諾廷加姆爾賓厄姆三世
zh-hans.Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, England: 英国多瑟特梅爾可姆 霍爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Bingham于大约1265年出生在英国多瑟特梅爾可姆 霍爾时，他的父亲Robert Bingham35岁，母亲Emma Or Eleanor Wake22岁。他于1290年与Parnelle Warmwell在英国英格兰多瑟特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1317年在英国英格兰享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Dorset) and Irish (County Mayo): habitational name from Bingham (Nottinghamshire). The placename is probably from an Old English folk-name Bynningas (‘the people associated with a man named Bynna’), or possibly from an unattested Old English word bing ‘a kettle-shaped hollow’, + Old English hām ‘homestead’.
Irish (Ulster, of Scottish origin): altered form of   Bigham  .
American shortened and altered form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames such as   Bingenheimer  .

variants.name: Ingham
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of the places called Ingham, particularly in Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Suffolk. The placename is from the Old English personal name Inga + hām ‘homestead’. In Yorkshire this name was sometimes confused with Bingham in the 18th century.
English (of Norman origin): variant of the obsolete Norman surname Engaine; see Gain .

variants.name: Bigham
meanings: Scottish (Wigtownshire) and Irish: habitational name from either Bigholm in Beith parish (Ayrshire) or Bigholms in Langholm parish (Dumfriesshire). The second element of the placename is from Older Scots holm (Old Norse holmr) ‘islet’, ‘raised land in a marsh’, ‘a stretch of low-lying land beside a river; a meadow’. The first element may be an unattested Older Scots bigge, a shortened form of bigging ‘building’. The name was taken to Ulster in the 17th and 18th centuries, where it was used interchangeably with Bingham .

variants.name: Bingenheimer
meanings: German and Jewish (from Germany): habitational name for someone from Bingenheim on the Eder river (Hesse). Compare   Bingham  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Bingham was born about 1265, in Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, England, his father, Robert Bingham, was 35 and his mother, Emma Or Eleanor Wake, was 22. He married Parnelle Warmwell in 1290, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died about 1317, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Bingham est né environ 1265, à Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Robert Bingham, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Emma Or Eleanor Wake, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Parnelle Warmwell en 1290, à Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1317, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Bingham um 1265 in Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Bingham 35 und seine Mutter Emma Or Eleanor Wake 22. Er hat 1290 in Dorset, England, United Kingdom Parnelle Warmwell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1317 im Alter von 54 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Bingham è nato il circa 1265 nel Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Robert Bingham, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Emma Or Eleanor Wake, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Parnelle Warmwell nel 1290, nel Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1317, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 54 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Bingham(이)가 대략 1265년 영국 도르세트 멜코브 호르시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Bingham(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma Or Eleanor Wake(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1290년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트에서 Parnelle Warmwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1317년 영국 잉글랜드에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Bingham nasceu aproximadamente 1265, em Melcombe Horsey, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Bingham, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Emma Or Eleanor Wake, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Parnelle Warmwell em 1290, em Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1317, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Bingham родился около 1265 г. в местности мелкомб хаурси, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Bingham было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma Or Eleanor Wake, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Parnelle Warmwell в 1290 г. в местности дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1317 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет.
